title: Tabora District Council (Community Health Fund) By-laws, 2001
title_en: Tabora District Council (Community Health Fund) By-laws, 2001

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These By-laws may be cited as the Tabora District Council (Com­munity Health Fund) By-laws, 2001.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These By-laws shall apply throughout the area of jurisdiction of the Tabora District Council .

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these By-laws unlesss the context otherwise requires— " Act " means the Local Government (District Authorities) Act, 1982; " Board " means a Council Health Services Board established in accor­dance with the provision of Section 86A of the Act ; " Card " means a Community Health Fund Card; " Council " means the Tabora District Council; " Contribution " means an annual voluntary contribution to the Fund; " District Medical Officer " means a Medical Officer in-charge of Tabora District Council Health Services; " Health Care Facility " means government or private health care facility and includes any other facility established or organized for purposed of delivering health services; " Household " means:— (a) a mother, father and children under the age of eighteen years; or (b) a member who has attained the age of eighteen years or more with or without children under the age of eighteen years or; (c) an institution; " Institution " means a registered school college; or any other body as may be approved by the Board ; " Member " means a contributing household to the Community Health Fund; " User Fee " means a fee charged by a health care facility for services rendered. [Act. No 7 of 1982]

provisions.title: Community Health Fund
provisions.content: (1) There shall be a Community Health Fund which is a volun­tary community based financing scheme whereby households pay con­tributions to finance part of their basic health care services to comple­ment the Government health care financing efforts. (2) Fund shall be managed by the Board in accordance with the provision of these By-laws.

provisions.title: Objectives of the Fund
provisions.content: The objectives of the Fund shall be:— (a) to mobilize financial resources from the community for pro­vision of health care services to its members; (b) to provide quality and affordable health care services through a sustainable financial mechanism; and (c) to improve health care services management in the communi­ties through decentralization by empowering the communi­ties in making decisions and by contributing on matters af­fecting their health.

provisions.title: Functions of the Board
provisions.content: In achieving its overall objectives the Board shall have the follow­ing functions:— (a) to provide district health services and monitor Community Health Fund operations and activities; (b) to work in consultation with the Council Health Management Team to ensure quality health care and professionalism; (c) to mobilize and administer funds for Community Health Fund; (d) to set exemption criteria for users of the health care services provided by the Fund; (e) to set targets for the Funds; (f) to review reports from Ward Health Committees or any other source; (g) to monitor and make verification on collection, expenditure and control of funds; and (h) to design an annual health plan for approval by the Council .

provisions.title: Sources of revenue of the Fund
provisions.content: The source of revenue of the Fund shall be: (a) all money received in respect of contributions paid by mem­bers. (b) grants from the Council , Central Government and donors; (c) donation from individuals who reside within or outside the area of jurisdiction of the Council ; (d) user charges payable for using health facilities which are un­der the management of the Council ; (e) such levies as maybe determined and assigned to the Fund by the Council from time to time; (f) any other money lawfully derived by the Fund from any other source not expressly specified in these By-laws.

provisions.title: Accounts of the Board
provisions.content: (1) There shall be kept proper accounts and other records in rela­tion to the resources of the Fund. (2) The Board shall open a separate bank account for the purposes of maintaining the revenues of the Fund from which moneys shall be deposited and drawn. (3) The signatories to the account of the Fund shall be appointed by the Board and shall constitute of category "A" and "B" and in no case moneys may be drawn from the Fund account without a signature from each of the two categories. (4) The signatories of the Fund shall be:— (a) the Chairperson of the Board and one member of the Board who shall be in category "A". (b) the Secretary of the Board who is a District Medical Officer and a medical officer in-charge of a Hospital who shall be in category "B". (5) In each financial year of the Council , the Board shall submit be­fore the Council a statement of its accounts in such form and detail as the Council may direct. (6) The accounts of the Fund shall be audited by auditors appointed in accordance with existing regulations governing auditing of the Coun­cil accounts.

provisions.title: Report of the Fund
provisions.content: The Board shall as soon as practicable after the end of each financial year of the Council prepare a full report on the performance by it of its functions during the financial year.

provisions.title: Planning and budgeting
provisions.content: (1) Two months before the start of the Council 's financial Board shall prepare a detailed plan and budget showing the amount expected to be received and disbursed to finance the delivery of health services for such development activities for the better provision of health serv­ices within the area of jurisdiction of the Council . (2) The Board may whenever circumstances so require, prepare a supplementary plan and budget. (3) The plan and budget of the Fund prepared in accordance with the provisions of paragraph (1) and (2) of this by-law shall be submitted to the Council for approval.

provisions.title: Composition of the Board
provisions.content: The Board shall be composed of: (a) four community services users of whom at least two shall be fe­male; (b) one representative, each from a non-profit voluntary agency and a private for-profit health care facility, appointed by the Council from amongst health care facilities which have entered into a Serv­ice Agreement with the Board ; (c) the head of the Council Social Services Committee; (d) the Council planning officer; (e) the District Medical Officer who shall be the Secretary to the Board ; (f) one representative from the hospital; and (g) one representative from the Regional Health Management Team.

provisions.title: Qualifications of members of Board
provisions.content: A person shall be qualified to be a member under paragraph (a) of By-law 11 if:— (a) he is a citizen; (b) he is of the age between 25 to 70 years; (c) he has at least completed primary school education from a regis­tered school; (d) has experience and proven abilities in social services; and (e) has leadership qualities.

provisions.title: Tenure of office of members
provisions.content: (1) A member of the Board shall hold office for a period of three years from the date of his nomination and may be re-elected for another one term only. (2) If any vacancy occurs in the membership of the Board by reason of death, resignation, permanent incapacity or for any other reason, an­other person may be nominated to fill the vacancy and a person so nomi­nated shall hold office for the un-expired residue period of office of the member in whose place he is nominated.

provisions.title: Management of the Fund
provisions.content: (1) The management and administration of the Fund shall vest:— (a) at district level; to the Council , through the Council Health Serv­ices Board ; (b) at ward level; to the Ward Development Committee, through the Ward Health Committee; (c) at village level; to the Village Council , through the Village Social Services Committee. (2) The Fund shall be under the overall management of the Board which shall be responsible for:— (a) coordinating the activities of the Fund; (b) monitoring, controlling and directing the management of the resources of the Fund; (c) ensuring that the Fund is effectively and efficiently used and managed.

provisions.title: Application of resources of the Fund
provisions.content: The Board shall apply the resources of the Fund for the payment of the services rendered, and shall be limited to the fulfilment of the objectives of the Fund and in so doing, be governed by principle for efficiency, effectiveness, economy and equity.

provisions.title: Membership and issuing of card
provisions.content: (1) Each member of the Fund shall be issued with a membership card upon paying the annual contribution which shall be proposed by the Ward Health Committee to the Council through the Board and approved on an annual basis by the Council . (2) Subject to the provisions of the Community Health Fund Regula­tions 2001, each exempted person, shall be issued with a membership card. [GN of 2001]

provisions.title: Provision of health service
provisions.content: Each member shall be entitled to receive a prepaid medical services from a pre-selected health care facility within the area of jurisdiction of the Council on the following basis:— (a) medical service to himself or to herself and his or her spouse and children of age of minority; (b) making proposals on the health care facility of his or her choice which shall be taken into account by the Board ; (c) involvement in the overall planning and implementation of health care services delivery in the area of jurisdiction of the Council .

provisions.title: Transfer of assets and liabilities
provisions.content: Notwithstanding the repeal of the Tabora District Council CHF By-law 19, all assets and liabilities which were previously vested in the CHF District Health Board under the repealed By-laws shall be deemed to be transferred to the Board .

provisions.title: Repeal
provisions.content: The Tabora District Council Community Health Fund By-laws is hereby repealed.
